issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator. This can hide focus from keyboard users if browser support varies.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile nav trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open, mobileNav.close, and mobileNav.toggle functions change the visible state of the navigation drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the expanded/collapsed state of the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 1843: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`. Line 1846: update aria-expanded based on the new state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on dropdown trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and dropdownMenu._closeInstance functions change the visible state of dropdown panels but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) for interactive controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.open = true;`. Line 1980: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates visible price amounts and labels when the user switches between monthly/annual billing, but does not announce the change to screen readers via an aria-live region. Users with visual impairments may not be aware of the update.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');` (or similar) after `_pricingState.period = period;`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visible product grid after filtering/sorting, but does not announce the count of visible products to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology will not know the results have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2750: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || 'Filter applied');` after the `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...)` call.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult function marks invalid fields with CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state, violating WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification).
issuesFound.fix: Line 4000: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` inside the invalid result block. Line 4003: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` inside the valid result block.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button (hamburger button) does not have an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element it controls. Screen reader users cannot programmatically determine the relationship between the trigger and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: after `_drawer = qs(SELECTORS.navDrawer...);`, add `if (_trigger && _drawer) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));`.

issuesFound.title: Carousel navigation buttons missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel previous/next buttons do not have aria-controls attributes pointing to the carousel track they control. This prevents screen reader users from understanding the relationship between the buttons and the content they affect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3650: after `_trackEl = qs('[data-carousel-track]', _rootEl);`, add `if (_prevBtn && _trackEl) setAttr(_prevBtn, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_trackEl, 'carousel-track'));` and similarly for _nextBtn.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically-hidden mobile drawer not updated in accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle CSS classes to show/hide the drawer but do not set aria-hidden on the drawer element. When hidden, the drawer remains in the accessibility tree, potentially causing confusion for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, false);` after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass...);`. Line 1843: add `setAttr(_drawer, ARIA.HIDDEN, true);` after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass...);`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons not updating aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (e.g., monthly/annual) act as a two-state toggle but do not set aria-pressed to reflect the selected state. Screen reader users cannot perceive which option is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3120: in the `_syncToggleState` function, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` for each period button.

issuesFound.title: Product view mode toggle buttons not updating aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) act as a two-state toggle but do not set aria-pressed to reflect the selected view. Screen reader users cannot perceive which view is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2790: in the `_onViewToggle` function, add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, true);` for the selected button and `setAttr(otherBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, false);` for the other.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open and faqAccordion.close functions expand/collapse FAQ answers but do not announce these state changes to screen readers via an aria-live region. Users may not know content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3400: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) || 'Answer expanded');`. Line 3410: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) || 'Answer collapsed');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and _closeInstance functions show/hide dropdown panels but do not announce these actions to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology may not be aware that a menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Menu opened');`. Line 1980: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions show/hide the mobile navigation drawer but do not announce these state changes to screen readers. Screen reader users may not know the menu is now available or has been dismissed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1840: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`. Line 1843: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set to "false" on page load when the drawer is closed. Screen reader users will not know the initial collapsed state of the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: after initialising `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded initialised to "false" on page load when their panels are hidden. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state is not programmatically determinable initially.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: in the `dropdownMenu.register` function, add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after setting aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: Cookie consent decision not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onAccept and _onDecline functions record the user's consent choice but do not announce the result to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not receive confirmation that their selection was applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4550: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.cookies.saved || 'Cookie preferences saved.');`. Line 4560: similarly add the same announcement.

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions change the visibility of the navigation drawer but never update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` (inside open), Line 1165: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` (inside close)

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod` function updates the prices and savings messages across the page, but there is no corresponding write to an `aria-live` region to announce that the billing period has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1413: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual);` (depending on the period selected)

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function adds a CSS class `CSS.fieldInvalid` and updates the error message, but it does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1523: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator._renderResult` function removes the invalid CSS class when a field becomes valid, but it does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1518: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function sets up the trigger but does not explicitly set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button for the initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1188: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dynamically-hidden element not updated in accessibility tree

issuesFound.fix: Line 564: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ accordion buttons remove their focus outline, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which question is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the footer brand section lose their focus outline, reducing navigability for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 802: `.footer-brand a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove their focus outline, making it hard for keyboard users to locate the focused link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 822: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links lose their focus outline, hindering keyboard navigation to those links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 842: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes its focus outline, so users cannot see when the region is focused for keyboard scrolling.
issuesFound.fix: Line 952: `.table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The “Learn more” link inside feature cards removes its focus outline, making it invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1022: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons lose their focus outline, preventing keyboard users from seeing which billing period is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1122: `.pricing-toggle button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the WCAG minimum touch target of 24 × 24 px, making it hard for users with limited dexterity to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons set `min-width: 20px; min-height: 20px`, both below the required 24 px minimum, reducing tap accuracy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 380: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: `word-spacing: -0.2em;` creates overlapping words, making text hard to read for all users, especially those with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 71: `word-spacing: 0;`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes underlines from all links without providing another visual cue (e.g., border‑bottom or font‑weight change), violating SC 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 78: `a { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` on hover, but the `prefers-reduced-motion` media query does not disable this animation, causing unwanted motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1004: `.hero-image img { transition: none; }` (add inside the existing `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section moves upward with `transform: translateY(-1px)` on hover, and there is no reduced‑motion override, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Add inside the existing `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block:  
  Line 960: `.hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (dropdown menu)
issuesFound.explanation: The internal function `_openInstance` in the dropdown menu controller changes the panel’s visibility (adds/removes the “open” class and hidden attribute) but never sets `aria-expanded` on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the menu state.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')` when opening and `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when closing.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (site search)
issuesFound.explanation: The functions `_open` and `_close` in the site‑search module show or hide the suggestion list but never set `aria-expanded` on the search `<input>` element, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_open` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `_close` call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: The `open(item)` function expands a FAQ panel and adds the “open” class, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button, while `close(item)` does set it to `false`. The missing `true` state prevents AT from announcing the open panel.
issuesFound.fix: After opening, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑expanded on page load (dropdown menu)
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is parsed, the trigger button receives `aria-haspopup` and `aria-controls`, but no `aria-expanded` attribute is set to reflect its initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: In `register()` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑expanded on page load (site search)
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` receives `aria-controls` and other ARIA attributes, but lacks an initial `aria-expanded="false"` value, so AT cannot know the combobox is collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the input (or in `siteSearch.init`), call `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Toggle trigger missing aria‑expanded on page load (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers are given `aria-controls` and `role="button"` but are not initialized with `aria-expanded="false"` when the panel is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In `faqRegistry.parse` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` for items that are not initially open.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button missing aria‑pressed (billing period switch)
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (element with `data-billing-toggle`) is given `role="switch"` and is keyboard‑operable, but its pressed state is never reflected via `aria-pressed`, breaking the ARIA switch pattern.
issuesFound.fix: In `_onToggleClick` (and on initialization) call `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-pressed', _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation does not set aria‑invalid (inline validator)
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate` routine marks fields visually but only adds `aria-describedby` via `srUtils.markInvalid`; it never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid inputs, so AT does not know the field is in error.
issuesFound.fix: In `_renderResult` when `!result.valid` add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true')`; when valid, remove or set `aria-invalid="false"`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden (dropdown menu)
issuesFound.explanation: `_closeInstance` adds the `hidden` attribute to the dropdown panel but does not also set `aria-hidden="true"`, leaving the element in the accessibility tree while invisible.
issuesFound.fix: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` add `setAttr(panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden (site‑search suggestions)
issuesFound.explanation: `suggestionListRenderer.hide` adds `hidden` to the listbox but does not set `aria-hidden="true"`, so screen readers may still read the hidden list.
issuesFound.fix: In `hide()` add `setAttr(_listEl, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; in `show()` remove it.

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not updated with aria‑hidden (FAQ accordion)
issuesFound.explanation: `close(item)` sets `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '')` but never adds `aria-hidden="true"` to the panel, violating the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding, call `setAttr(item.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true')`; when opening, remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content change not announced via live region (dropdown menu)
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown changes visual state (adds/removes “open” class) but no live‑region announcement is made, so AT users are unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After `_openInstance` or `_closeInstance`, call `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened')` or `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed')`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content change not announced via live region (pricing period change)
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod` updates price numbers on all plan cards, but does not announce the new pricing to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the UI, call `liveRegion.announce('Pricing switched to ' + periodLabel)`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content change not announced via live region (sticky header hide/show)
issuesFound.explanation: `stickyHeader._onScroll` adds/removes the “header‑hidden” class to hide the header, but no live‑region message is emitted, leaving AT users unaware of the header’s disappearance.
issuesFound.fix: After hiding/showing, call `liveRegion.announce('Header hidden')` or `liveRegion.announce('Header shown')`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content change not announced via live region (product filter count update)
issuesFound.explanation: `productFilter.applyFilters` updates the product count badge and empty‑state message, but does not provide a live‑region update, so AT users cannot know how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count, call `liveRegion.announce(count + ' products found')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu panels do not reflect visibility with `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: `_openInstance()` removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but never sets `aria‑hidden="false"`. `_closeInstance()` adds `hidden` but does not set `aria‑hidden="true"`. Screen readers may still expose hidden panels.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown toggle buttons do not update `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The button that opens a dropdown never receives an `aria‑expanded` update, so its expanded/collapsed state is not conveyed to AT.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion items do not update `aria‑expanded` on toggle
issuesFound.explanation: `faqAccordion.open()` and `faqAccordion.close()` animate the panel but never set `aria‑expanded` on the question button, leaving the state unknown to assistive tech.

issuesFound.title: Search input does not reflect suggestion panel state via `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The search `<input>` gets `aria‑controls` but never receives `aria‑expanded="true"` when the suggestion list is shown, nor `false` when hidden.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons do not update `aria‑pressed`
issuesFound.explanation: Period toggle buttons (`_periodBtns`) receive visual `active` class changes but never set `aria‑pressed`, which is required for two‑state toggle controls.

issuesFound.title: Inline validation does not set `aria‑invalid` on error fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the code calls `srUtils.markInvalid()` which only adds `aria-describedby`. It never sets `aria-invalid="true"`, so screen readers cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open/close actions are not announced to AT
issuesFound.explanation: After toggling a FAQ item, no live‑region announcement is made, leaving users unaware of the change.

issuesFound.title: Product filter updates count badge without live‑region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters change, the visible count updates but no ARIA live region informs screen‑reader users of the new result count.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown panels are hidden with `hidden` attribute but lack `aria‑hidden`
issuesFound.explanation: Without `aria‑hidden="true"` assistive tech may still expose the panel.
issuesFound.fix:
As in Issue 2, set `aria-hidden` appropriately when showing/hiding panels.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required‑field marker missing aria‑hidden (Name field)

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing associated label
issuesFound.explanation: The email <input> has an id but the corresponding <label> in FormGroup does not use htmlFor, so the input is not programmatically labelled.
issuesFound.fix: In FormGroup component, change <label className="form-label"> to <label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label"> and keep the existing label text.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Full name” input missing associated label
issuesFound.explanation: The <input> for full name has an id but the <label> rendered by FormGroup does not associate via htmlFor, leaving the field unlabeled for AT.
issuesFound.fix:
Update FormGroup as in Issue 21 to use htmlFor={htmlFor} on the <label> element.

(Repeat Issue 22 for Email, Subject, and Message inputs – lines 1170, 1185, 1200 – same fix.)

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input missing aria‑invalid when in error state
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the input receives an “invalid” prop but the component does not render aria‑invalid, so AT is not informed of the error.
issuesFound.fix:
In TextInput component, add aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} to the <input> element.

(Repeat Issue 23 for Contact form inputs – lines 1155, 1170, 1185, 1200 – same fix.)

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input error message not linked via aria‑describedby
issuesFound.explanation: The input’s aria‑describedby points to an id that is never rendered because the ErrorMessage component is not used, leaving the error message inaccessible.
issuesFound.fix:
Render <ErrorMessage id="{emailErrorId}" message={email.state.error} /> inside the FormGroup (or pass errorId and errorMessage props to FormGroup) so the referenced id exists.

(Repeat Issue 24 for each Contact form field – lines 1155, 1170, 1185, 1200 – same fix.)

issuesFound.title: Newsletter submit button missing aria‑busy during loading

issuesFound.fix: Line 1052: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting} aria-describedby={statusId}>…</Button>

issuesFound.title: Contact form submit button missing aria‑busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button displays a loading state without aria‑busy, so AT cannot detect that the button is busy.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1225: <Button type="submit" variant="primary" size="md" loading={isSubmitting} aria-busy={isSubmitting} aria-disabled={isSubmitting}>…</Button>

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or non-zero outline. This removes the keyboard focus ring entirely, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify which element is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 336: outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only uses outline-offset. Offset applies to a zero-width outline, rendering nothing. Keyboard users cannot see focus on buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement. The :focus state (Line 567) uses box-shadow, which is invisible in Windows High Contrast Mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input:focus selector sets outline: none and relies solely on box-shadow. Box-shadows are suppressed in Forced Colors Mode, leaving high-contrast users without a focus indicator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1123: outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 2px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove the outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus within the footer navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in High Contrast Mode on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside @media (forced-colors: active), the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none. This actively breaks focus visibility for users relying on High Contrast Mode.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: outline: 2px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 2px;

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform, which causes spatial movement. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (Line 409) is empty and does not disable this animation.

issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset. An offset on a zero-width outline renders nothing, removing the focus ring entirely for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes the content fully visible on screen instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern for skip links and labels.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses text and significantly reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision. WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing recommends allowing standard spacing.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), and the active state applies transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion, which may cause issues for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 432: Add to existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block or create new: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn:active applies transform: translateY(1px) causing spatial movement. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transform for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 437: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn:active { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .hero-image img includes transform in its transition and applies transform: perspective(...) on hover, causing 3D spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for this selector is empty (lines 766-769), providing no override to disable the animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 766-769: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none !important; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none !important; } }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown and hidden by setting the hidden attribute and toggling CSS classes, but the trigger button's aria-expanded state is never synchronised. Assistive technologies cannot announce whether the dropdown is open or closed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ answer panels are expanded and collapsed using animateHeight and the hidden attribute, but the question button's aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the current state. Screen readers cannot convey whether an answer is visible.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period buttons act as a toggle group where one button is always active, but aria-pressed is never set to indicate the pressed state. Screen readers cannot identify which billing period is currently selected.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the function adds an error message and links it with aria-describedby, but it never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an invalid value.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function removes the error message from aria-describedby but leaves aria-invalid="true" set. Screen readers continue to announce the field as invalid even after the error is corrected.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initialises, the trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set to indicate that the closed drawer is initially collapsed. Screen readers may not convey the correct initial state.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are not initialised with aria-expanded="false" to reflect that the dropdown panel is initially closed. Screen readers may not convey the correct initial state.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the search module initialises, the combobox input does not have aria-expanded="false" set to indicate that the suggestion list is initially collapsed. Screen readers may not convey the correct initial state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation opens or closes, no announcement is made to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. Users are not informed that the navigation state has changed.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that a menu has appeared or disappeared.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items expand or collapse, no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that content has been shown or hidden.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no announcement is made to screen readers regarding the number of results or the active filter. Users are not informed that the content has changed.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched between monthly and annual, prices update visually but no announcement is made to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices have changed or which billing period is now active.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer not removed from accessibility tree when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When the navigation drawer is closed, it is hidden via CSS class removal but aria-hidden="true" is not explicitly set. While CSS may hide the element, explicitly removing it from the accessibility tree ensures screen readers do not access the content when the drawer is closed.

fpTitles: dropdownMenu register does not initialise aria-expanded
dropdownMenu state change not announced via live region
faqAccordion state change not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance and _closeInstance do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown panels open or close, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated to reflect the visibility state of the controlled panel, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3355: Add after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`:
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line 3369: Add after `if (!inst.open) inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`:
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open and close functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion trigger buttons toggle panel visibility but fail to update their aria-expanded attributes, leaving screen reader users unaware of the current expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5555: Add after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`:
  `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 5580: Add after `item.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`:
  `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch _open and _close do not update aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not update aria-expanded when the suggestions list opens or closes, preventing assistive technologies from announcing the popup state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3439: Add after `_searchState.open = true;`:
  `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 3450: Add after `_searchState.open = false;`:
  `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState does not update aria-pressed on billing toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) function as toggle buttons but do not set aria-pressed to indicate which period is currently active, failing to convey the selected state to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked invalid, the function only updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers cannot identify the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1809: Add after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`:
  `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are cleared or validated successfully, the aria-invalid attribute is not removed or set to false, potentially leaving stale invalid states announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1820: Add after the existing logic in markValid:
  `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not set aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks an aria-controls attribute pointing to the drawer element, breaking the programmatic association required for the disclosure pattern.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not set aria-haspopup on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button lacks aria-haspopup to indicate it controls a menu popup, which is required for the disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3285: Add:
  `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.HASPOPUP, 'menu');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are registered without an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute, leaving the initial state ambiguous for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3395: Add after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`:
  `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not initialise aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is created without an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute, failing to indicate that the suggestions popup is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3389: Add after `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`:
  `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce changes via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the visible card count changes but no announcement is made to screen reader users, leaving them unaware that the filter action succeeded or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4555: Add inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`:
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) || `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users, nor is the savings information conveyed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function ignores announceToSR parameter and does not announce
issuesFound.explanation: Although the open() function accepts an announceToSR parameter, it never uses it to make a live region announcement. Opening FAQ items is not conveyed to screen reader users unless they manually check the content.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function does not announce via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are collapsed, no announcement is made to screen reader users, leaving them unaware that the content has been hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 5580: Add before `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`:
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.title) || `${item.title} collapsed`);`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick does not announce scroll action
issuesFound.explanation: When users activate the scroll-to-top button, the page scrolls but the action is not announced to screen reader users, potentially causing disorientation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3555: Add at the end of the function (before the closing brace):
  `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');`

fpTitles: mobileNav init does not set aria-haspopup on trigger
dropdownMenu register does not initialise aria-expanded
faqAccordion open function ignores announceToSR parameter and does not announce
faqAccordion close function does not announce via live region
scrollToTop _onClick does not announce scroll action

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded and aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, the mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set, and it lacks aria-controls linking it to the drawer element. This prevents screen readers from understanding the initial state and the relationship between the button and the drawer.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1 (after `_initialised = true;`): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`
  Line 2 (after ensuring _drawer has an id): Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu panels are shown and hidden by removing or adding the hidden attribute, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded state is never updated. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the state is not programmatically determined.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1 (in _openInstance, after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
  Line 2 (in _closeInstance, after `inst.open = false;`): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: While the close function sets aria-expanded="false", the open function fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button when expanding an FAQ item. This leaves screen readers unaware that the panel is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in open, after `item.open = true;`): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox does not toggle aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox with an expandable suggestion list, but it never updates aria-expanded to reflect whether the listbox is open or closed. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state correctly.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1 (in _open, after `_searchState.open = true;`): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line 2 (in _close, after `_searchState.open = false;`): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The combobox input does not have aria-expanded="false" set initially when the suggestion list is closed on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in init, after `suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markInvalid, after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field transitions from invalid to valid, the function removes the error description but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute, leaving stale invalid state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in markValid, after updating aria-describedby): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible content significantly, but no announcement is made to screen readers via the liveRegion utility. Users are not informed that the navigation has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1 (in open, after emitting event): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line 2 (in close, after emitting event): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes the content dynamically, but screen readers are not notified via a live region. Users may not realize that new content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1 (in open, after emitting event): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.faqOpened || '{title} expanded').replace('{title}', item.question));`
  Line 2 (in close, after emitting event): Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.announce.faqClosed || '{title} collapsed').replace('{title}', item.question));`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing updates the prices visually, but screen readers are not informed of the change in billing period or prices via a live region announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in setPeriod, after updating prices): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not managed on open/close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved to the drawer or the first focusable element within it. When closed, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the document.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1 (in open, after `_lockScroll();`): Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`
  Line 2 (in close, after `_unlockScroll();`): Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: dynamically hidden elements missing aria-hidden attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When these components hide content (mobile drawer, dropdown panels, scroll-to-top button), they rely solely on CSS or the hidden attribute. While the hidden attribute is present in some cases, aria-hidden="true" is not explicitly set to ensure robust accessibility tree removal in all browser/AT combinations.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1 (in mobileNav.close, after `removeClass(_drawer, ...)`): Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Line 2 (in mobileNav.open): Add `removeAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden');`
  Line 3 (in dropdownMenu._closeInstance, after `inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`): Add `setAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden', 'true');`
  Line 4 (in dropdownMenu._openInstance): Add `removeAttr(inst.panel, 'aria-hidden');`
